title: [FIRRTL] better wire canonicalization
body:
```firrtl
FIRRTL version 4.1.0
circuit Foo:
  public module Foo:
    input clock : Clock
    input in : UInt<1>
    output out : UInt<1>

    wire w : Probe<UInt<1>>
    define w = probe(in)
    connect out, read(w)
```

gives

```verilog
// Generated by CIRCT firtool-1.88.0-382-g0276e17b2
module Foo(
  input  clock,
         in,
  output out
);

  wire in_probe = in;
  assign out = Foo.in_probe;
endmodule
```

I would have expected the probe to have been removed entirely.

Removing the intermediate wire gives the expected result:
```firrtl
FIRRTL version 4.1.0
circuit Foo:
  public module Foo:
    input clock : Clock
    input in : UInt<1>
    output out : UInt<1>

    connect out, read(probe(in))
```
```verilog
// Generated by CIRCT firtool-1.88.0-382-g0276e17b2
module Foo(
  input  clock,
         in,
  output out
);

  assign out = in;
endmodule
```
